items.title: Tigon: A Distributed Database for a CXL Pod.
Tintin: A Unified Hardware Performance Profiling Infrastructure to Uncover and Manage Uncertainty.
WLB-LLM: Workload-Balanced 4D Parallelism for Large Language Model Training.
Paralegal: Practical Static Analysis for Privacy Bugs.
Okapi: Decoupling Data Striping and Redundancy Grouping in Cluster File Systems.
Low End-to-End Latency atop a Speculative Shared Log with Fix-Ante Ordering.
EMT: An OS Framework for New Memory Translation Architectures.
Fork in the Road: Reflections and Optimizations for Cold Start Latency in Production Serverless Systems.
PipeThreader: Software-Defined Pipelining for Efficient DNN Execution.
Decentralized, Epoch-based F2FS Journaling with Fine-grained Crash Recovery.
Kamino: Efficient VM Allocation at Scale with Latency-Driven Cache-Aware Scheduling.
QiMeng-Xpiler: Transcompiling Tensor Programs for Deep Learning Systems with a Neural-Symbolic Approach.
items.abstract: Heterogeneous deep learning systems (DLS) such as GPUs and ASICs have been widely deployed in industrial data centers, which requires to develop multiple low-level tensor programs for different platforms. An attractive solution to relieve the programming burden is to transcompile the legacy code of one platform to others. However, current transcompilation techniques struggle with either tremendous manual efforts or functional incorrectness, rendering "Write Once, Run Anywhere" of tensor programs an open question. We propose a novel transcompiler, i.e., QiMeng-Xpiler, for automatically translating tensor programs across DLS via both large language models (LLMs) and symbolic program synthesis, i.e., neural-symbolic synthesis. The key insight is leveraging the powerful code generation ability of LLM to make costly search-based symbolic synthesis computationally tractable. Concretely, we propose multiple LLM-assisted compilation passes via pre-defined meta-prompts for program transformation. During each program transformation, efficient symbolic program synthesis is employed to repair incorrect code snippets with a limited scale. To attain high performance, we propose a hierarchical auto-tuning approach to systematically explore both the parameters and sequences of transformation passes. Experiments on 4 DLS with distinct programming interfaces, i.e., Intel DL Boost with VNNI, NVIDIA GPU with CUDA, AMD MI with HIP, and Cambricon MLU with BANG, demonstrate that QiMeng-Xpiler correctly translates different tensor programs at the accuracy of 95% on average, and the performance of translated programs achieves up to 2.0x over vendor-provided manually-optimized libraries. As a result, the programming productivity of DLS is improved by up to 96.0x via transcompiling legacy tensor programs.
items.abstract_zh: GPU和ASIC等异构深度学习系统（DLS）已广泛部署在工业数据中心中，这需要为不同平台开发多个低级张量程序。减轻编程负担的一种有吸引力的解决方案是将一个平台的遗留代码转编译到其他平台。然而，当前的转编译技术要么面临巨大的手动工作，要么遇到功能错误，使得张量程序的“一次编写，随处运行”成为一个悬而未决的问题。我们提出了一种新颖的转编译器，即 QiMeng-Xpiler，用于通过大语言模型（LLM）和符号程序合成（即神经符号合成）在 DLS 上自动翻译张量程序。关键的见解是利用法学硕士强大的代码生成能力，使昂贵的基于搜索的符号合成在计算上易于处理。具体来说，我们通过预定义的元提示来建议多个 LLM 辅助编译过程以进行程序转换。在每次程序转换过程中，都会采用高效的符号程序合成来修复有限范围内的错误代码片段。为了获得高性能，我们提出了一种分层自动调整方法来系统地探索变换过程的参数和序列。在具有不同编程接口的 4 个 DLS（即带有 VNNI 的 Intel DL Boost、带有 CUDA 的 NVIDIA GPU、带有 HIP 的 AMD MI 和带有 BANG 的寒武纪 MLU）上进行的实验表明，QiMeng-Xpiler 能够以平均 95% 的准确率正确翻译不同的张量程序，并且翻译后的程序的性能比供应商提供的手动优化库高出 2.0 倍。因此，通过转编译遗留张量程序，DLS 的编程效率提高了高达 96.0 倍。

items.title: Picsou: Enabling Replicated State Machines to Communicate Efficiently.
items.abstract: Replicated state machines (RSMs) cannot communicate effectively today as there is no formal framework or efficient protocol to do so. To address this issue, we introduce a new primitive, Cross-Cluster Consistent Broadcast (C3B) and present PICSOU, a practical implementation of the C3B primitive. PICSOU draws inspiration from networking and TCP to allow two RSMs to communicate with constant metadata overhead in the failure-free case and a minimal number of message resends in the case of failures. PICSOU is flexible and allows both crash fault tolerant and Byzantine fault tolerant consensus protocols to communicate. At the heart of PICSOU's good performance and generality is the concept of QUACKs (quorum acknowledgments). QUACKs allow nodes in each RSM to precisely determine when messages have definitely been received, or likely lost. Our results are promising: we obtain up to 24x better performance than prior solutions on microbenchmarks and applications, ranging from disaster recovery to data reconciliation.
items.abstract_zh: 如今，复制状态机 (RSM) 无法有效地进行通信，因为没有正式的框架或有效的协议来实现这一点。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了一种新的原语，跨集群一致广播（C3B），并提出了 PICSOU，这是 C3B 原语的实际实现。 PICSOU 从网络和 TCP 中汲取灵感，允许两个 RSM 在无故障情况下以恒定的元数据开销进行通信，并在发生故障时以最少数量的消息重新发送。 PICSOU 非常灵活，允许崩溃容错和拜占庭容错共识协议进行通信。 PICSOU 良好性能和通用性的核心是 QUACK（法定人数确认）的概念。 QUACK 允许每个 RSM 中的节点精确确定消息何时已明确收到或可能丢失。我们的结果令人鼓舞：我们在微基准测试和应用程序（从灾难恢复到数据协调）方面获得了比以前的解决方案高出 24 倍的性能。

items.title: Stripeless Data Placement for Erasure-Coded In-Memory Storage.
QOS: Quantum Operating System.
items.abstract: Quantum computers face challenges due to hardware constraints, noise errors, and heterogeneity, and face fundamental design tradeoffs between key performance metrics such as \textit{quantum fidelity} and system utilization. This substantially complicates managing quantum resources to scale the size and number of quantum algorithms that can be executed reliably in a given time. We introduce QOS, a cloud operating system for managing quantum resources while mitigating their inherent limitations and balancing the design tradeoffs of quantum computing. QOS exposes a hardware-agnostic API for transparent quantum job execution, mitigates hardware errors, and systematically multi-programs and schedules the jobs across space and time to achieve high quantum fidelity in a resource-efficient manner. To achieve this, it leverages two key insights: First, to maximize utilization and minimize fidelity loss, some jobs are more compatible than others for multi-programming on the same quantum computer. Second, sacrificing minimal fidelity can significantly reduce job waiting times. We evaluate QOS on real quantum devices hosted by IBM, using 7000 real quantum runs of more than 70.000 benchmark instances. We show that the QOS achieves 2.6--456.5$\times$ higher fidelity, increases resource utilization by up to 9.6$\times$, and reduces waiting times by up to 5$\times$ while sacrificing only 1--3\% fidelity, on average, compared to the baselines.
items.abstract_zh: 量子计算机面临着由于硬件限制、噪声误差和异质性而带来的挑战，并且面临着关键性能指标（例如\textit{量子保真度}）和系统利用率之间的基本设计权衡。这使得管理量子资源变得更加复杂，以扩展可以在给定时间内可靠执行的量子算法的大小和数量。我们引入了 QOS，这是一种云操作系统，用于管理量子资源，同时减轻其固有的局限性并平衡量子计算的设计权衡。 QOS 公开了一个与硬件无关的 API，用于透明的量子作业执行，减少硬件错误，并系统地跨空间和时间进行多程序和调度作业，以资源高效的方式实现高量子保真度。为了实现这一目标，它利用了两个关键的见解：首先，为了最大限度地提高利用率并最大限度地减少保真度损失，某些工作比其他工作更适合在同一台量子计算机上进行多重编程。其次，牺牲最低保真度可以显着减少作业等待时间。我们使用超过 70,000 个基准实​​例的 7000 次真实量子运行，在 IBM 托管的真实量子设备上评估 QOS。我们表明，与基线相比，QOS 的保真度平均提高了 2.6--456.5$\times$，资源利用率提高了高达 9.6$\times$，并减少了高达 5$\times$ 的等待时间，同时平均只牺牲了 1--3\% 的保真度。

items.title: KPerfIR: Towards a Open and Compiler-centric Ecosystem for GPU Kernel Performance Tooling on Modern AI Workloads.
items.abstract: In this work, we propose KPerfIR, a novel multilevel compiler-centric infrastructure to enable the development of customizable, extendable, and portable profiling tools tailored for modern artificial intelligence (AI) workloads on modern GPUs. Our approach integrates profiling capabilities directly into the compiler workflow, allowing profiling functionalities to be implemented as compiler passes, offering a programmable and reusable framework for performance analysis. This design bridges the gap between compilers and profilers, enabling fine-grained insights into complex optimization challenges such as overlapping the execution of fine-grained function units on GPUs. KPerfIR is integrated into the Triton infrastructure to highlight the power of a compiler-centric approach to advance performance analysis and optimization in the ever-evolving landscape of AI compilers. Our evaluation shows that our tool incurs low overhead (8.2%), provides accurate measurements (2% relative error), and delivers actionable insights into complicated GPU intra-kernel optimizations.
items.abstract_zh: 在这项工作中，我们提出了 KPerfIR，这是一种新颖的以编译器为中心的多级基础架构，能够开发针对现代 GPU 上的现代人工智能 (AI) 工作负载量身定制的可定制、可扩展和便携式分析工具。我们的方法将分析功能直接集成到编译器工作流程中，允许在编译器传递时实现分析功能，从而为性能分析提供可编程且可重用的框架。这种设计弥合了编译器和分析器之间的差距，能够对复杂的优化挑战进行细粒度的洞察，例如在 GPU 上重叠细粒度功能单元的执行。 KPerfIR 集成到 Triton 基础设施中，以突出以编译器为中心的方法的强大功能，以在不断发展的 AI 编译器领域推进性能分析和优化。我们的评估表明，我们的工具开销较低 (8.2%)，提供准确的测量（2% 相对误差），并为复杂的 GPU 内核内优化提供可操作的见解。

items.title: Achieving Low-Latency Graph-Based Vector Search via Aligning Best-First Search Algorithm with SSD.
items.abstract: We propose PipeANN , an on-disk graph-based approximate nearest neighbor search (ANNS) system, which significantly bridges the latency gap with in-memory ones. We achieve this by aligning the best-first search algorithm with SSD characteristics, avoiding strict compute-I/O order across search steps. Experiments show that PipeANN has 1.14 × –2.02 × search latency compared to in-memory Vamana, and 35.0% of the latency of on-disk DiskANN in billion-scale datasets, without sacrificing search accuracy. PipeANN is open-source at https://github.com/thustorage/PipeANN.
items.abstract_zh: 我们提出了 PipeANN ，这是一种基于磁盘图的近似最近邻搜索（ANNS）系统，它显着地弥补了与内存中的延迟差距。我们通过将最佳优先搜索算法与 SSD 特性结合起来，避免跨搜索步骤的严格计算 I/O 顺序来实现这一目标。实验表明，与内存中的 Vamana 相比，PipeANN 的搜索延迟为 1.14 × –2.02 ×，在十亿级数据集上的延迟为磁盘 DiskANN 的 35.0%，且不牺牲搜索精度。 PipeANN 是开源的，位于 https://github.com/thustorage/PipeANN。

items.title: WaferLLM: Large Language Model Inference at Wafer Scale.
items.abstract: Emerging AI accelerators increasingly adopt wafer-scale manufacturing technologies, integrating hundreds of thousands of AI cores in a mesh architecture with large distributed on-chip memory (tens of GB in total) and ultra-high on-chip memory bandwidth (tens of PB/s). However, current LLM inference systems, optimized for shared memory architectures like GPUs, fail to exploit these accelerators fully. We introduce WaferLLM, the first wafer-scale LLM inference system. WaferLLM is guided by a novel PLMR model (pronounced as "Plummer") that captures the unique hardware characteristics of wafer-scale architectures. Leveraging this model, WaferLLM pioneers wafer-scale LLM parallelism, optimizing the utilization of hundreds of thousands of on-chip cores. It also introduces MeshGEMM and MeshGEMV, the first GEMM and GEMV implementations designed to scale effectively on wafer-scale accelerators. Evaluations show that WaferLLM achieves up to 200$\times$ higher accelerator utilization than state-of-the-art methods. Leveraging a wafer-scale accelerator (Cerebras WSE2), WaferLLM delivers GEMV operations 606$\times$ faster and 16$\times$ more energy-efficient than on an NVIDIA A100 GPU. For full LLM inference, WaferLLM achieves 10-20$\times$ speedups over A100 GPU clusters running SGLang and vLLM. These advantages are expected to grow as wafer-scale AI models, software, and hardware continue to mature. WaferLLM is open-sourced at https://github.com/MeshInfra/WaferLLM.
items.abstract_zh: 新兴AI加速器越来越多地采用晶圆级制造技术，在网状架构中集成数十万个AI核心，具有大型分布式片上存储器（总计数十GB）和超高片上存储器带宽（数十PB/s）。然而，当前针对 GPU 等共享内存架构进行优化的 LLM 推理系统无法充分利用这些加速器。我们推出了 WaferLLM，这是第一个晶圆级 LLM 推理系统。 WaferLLM 以新颖的 PLMR 模型（发音为“Plummer”）为指导，该模型捕获了晶圆级架构的独特硬件特征。利用该模型，WaferLLM 开创了晶圆级 LLM 并行性，优化了数十万个片上内核的利用率。它还引入了 MeshGEMM 和 MeshGEMV，这是第一个旨在在晶圆级加速器上有效扩展的 GEMM 和 GEMV 实现。评估表明，WaferLLM 的加速器利用率比最先进的方法高出 200 美元\倍$。利用晶圆级加速器 (Cerebras WSE2)，WaferLLM 的 GEMV 操作速度比 NVIDIA A100 GPU 快 606 美元\倍，能效高 16 美元\倍。对于完整的 LLM 推理，WaferLLM 比运行 SGLang 和 vLLM 的 A100 GPU 集群实现了 10-20$\times$ 的加速。随着晶圆级人工智能模型、软件和硬件的不断成熟，这些优势预计将不断增强。 WaferLLM 在 https://github.com/MeshInfra/WaferLLM 上开源。

items.title: Neutrino: Fine-grained GPU Kernel Profiling via Programmable Probing.
Bayesian Code Diffusion for Efficient Automatic Deep Learning Program Optimization.
Training with Confidence: Catching Silent Errors in Deep Learning Training with Automated Proactive Checks.
items.abstract: Training deep learning (DL) models is a complex process, making it prone to silent errors that are challenging to detect and diagnose. This paper presents TRAINCHECK, a framework that takes a proactive checking approach to address silent training errors. TRAINCHECK automatically infers invariants tailored for DL training. It uses these invariants to proactively detect silent errors during the training process while providing debugging help. To evaluate TRAINCHECK, we reproduce 20 real-world silent training errors with diverse root causes. TRAINCHECK successfully detects 18 errors within a single training iteration. It also uncovers 6 unknown bugs in popular training libraries that lead to silent errors.
items.abstract_zh: 训练深度学习 (DL) 模型是一个复杂的过程，很容易出现难以检测和诊断的无声错误。本文提出了 TRAINCHECK，这是一个采用主动检查方法来解决静默训练错误的框架。 TRAINCHECK 自动推断为 DL 训练量身定制的不变量。它使用这些不变量在训练过程中主动检测无声错误，同时提供调试帮助。为了评估 TRAINCHECK，我们重现了 20 个具有不同根本原因的现实世界静默训练错误。 TRAINCHECK 在单次训练迭代中成功检测到 18 个错误。它还发现了流行训练库中导致静默错误的 6 个未知错误。

items.title: PoWER Never Corrupts: Tool-Agnostic Verification of Crash Consistency and Corruption Detection.
Understanding Stragglers in Large Model Training Using What-if Analysis.
items.abstract: Large language model (LLM) training is one of the most demanding distributed computations today, often requiring thousands of GPUs with frequent synchronization across machines. Such a workload pattern makes it susceptible to stragglers, where the training can be stalled by few slow workers. At ByteDance we find stragglers are not trivially always caused by hardware failures, but can arise from multiple complex factors. This work aims to present a comprehensive study on the straggler issues in LLM training, using a five-month trace collected from our ByteDance LLM training cluster. The core methodology is what-if analysis that simulates the scenario without any stragglers and contrasts with the actual case. We use this method to study the following questions: (1) how often do stragglers affect training jobs, and what effect do they have on job performance; (2) do stragglers exhibit temporal or spatial patterns; and (3) what are the potential root causes for stragglers?
items.abstract_zh: 大型语言模型 (LLM) 训练是当今要求最高的分布式计算之一，通常需要数千个 GPU，并且需要在机器之间频繁同步。这种工作负载模式很容易受到掉队者的影响，少数速度慢的员工可能会导致培训停滞。在字节跳动，我们发现落后者并不总是由硬件故障引起，而是可能由多种复杂因素引起。这项工作旨在利用从字节跳动 LLM 培训集群收集的五个月跟踪数据，对 LLM 培训中的落后者问题进行全面研究。核心方法是假设分析，模拟没有任何落后者的场景并与实际情况进行对比。我们使用这种方法来研究以下问题：（1）掉队者影响培训工作的频率如何，以及他们对工作绩效有何影响； (2) 落后者是否表现出时间或空间模式； (3) 落后者的潜在根本原因是什么？

items.title: Tiered Memory Management Beyond Hotness.
Deriving Semantic Checkers from Tests to Detect Silent Failures in Production Distributed Systems.
Skybridge: Bounded Staleness for Distributed Caches.
MettEagle: Costs and Benefits of Implementing Containers on Microkernels.
Quake: Adaptive Indexing for Vector Search.
items.abstract: Vector search, the task of finding the k-nearest neighbors of a query vector against a database of high-dimensional vectors, underpins many machine learning applications, including retrieval-augmented generation, recommendation systems, and information retrieval. However, existing approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) methods perform poorly under dynamic and skewed workloads where data distributions evolve. We introduce Quake, an adaptive indexing system that maintains low latency and high recall in such environments. Quake employs a multi-level partitioning scheme that adjusts to updates and changing access patterns, guided by a cost model that predicts query latency based on partition sizes and access frequencies. Quake also dynamically sets query execution parameters to meet recall targets using a novel recall estimation model. Furthermore, Quake utilizes NUMA-aware intra-query parallelism for improved memory bandwidth utilization during search. To evaluate Quake, we prepare a Wikipedia vector search workload and develop a workload generator to create vector search workloads with configurable access patterns. Our evaluation shows that on dynamic workloads, Quake achieves query latency reductions of 1.5-38x and update latency reductions of 4.5-126x compared to state-of-the-art indexes such as SVS, DiskANN, HNSW, and SCANN.
items.abstract_zh: 向量搜索是针对高维向量数据库查找查询向量的 k 最近邻的任务，它支撑着许多机器学习应用，包括检索增强生成、推荐系统和信息检索。然而，现有的近似最近邻（ANN）方法在数据分布不断变化的动态和倾斜工作负载下表现不佳。我们推出了 Quake，这是一种自适应索引系统，可以在此类环境中保持低延迟和高召回率。 Quake 采用多级分区方案，可根据更新和不断变化的访问模式进行调整，并以成本模型为指导，该模型根据分区大小和访问频率预测查询延迟。 Quake 还使用新颖的召回估计模型动态设置查询执行参数以满足召回目标。此外，Quake 利用 NUMA 感知的查询内并行性来提高搜索期间的内存带宽利用率。为了评估 Quake，我们准备了一个维基百科矢量搜索工作负载，并开发了一个工作负载生成器来创建具有可配置访问模式的矢量搜索工作负载。我们的评估表明，在动态工作负载上，与 SVS、DiskANN、HNSW 和 SCANN 等最先进的索引相比，Quake 的查询延迟降低了 1.5-38 倍，更新延迟降低了 4.5-126 倍。

items.title: Fast and Synchronous Crash Consistency with Metadata Write-Once File System.
Principles and Methodologies for Serial Performance Optimization.
DecDEC: A Systems Approach to Advancing Low-Bit LLM Quantization.
items.abstract: Quantization of Large Language Models (LLMs) has recently gained popularity, particularly for on-device settings with limited hardware resources. While efficient, quantization inevitably degrades model quality, especially in aggressive low-bit settings such as 3-bit and 4-bit precision. In this paper, we propose DecDEC, an inference scheme that improves the quality of low-bit LLMs while preserving the key benefits of quantization: GPU memory savings and latency reduction. DecDEC stores the residual matrix -- the difference between full-precision and quantized weights -- in CPU, and dynamically fetches the residuals for only a small portion of the weights. This portion corresponds to the salient channels, marked by activation outliers, with the fetched residuals helping to correct quantization errors in these channels. Salient channels are identified dynamically at each decoding step by analyzing the input activations -- this enables adaptation to the dynamic nature of activation distribution, thus maximizing the effectiveness of error compensation. We demonstrate the effectiveness of DecDEC by augmenting state-of-the-art quantization methods. For example, DecDEC reduces the perplexity of a 3-bit Llama-3-8B-Instruct model from 10.15 to 9.12 -- outperforming its 3.5-bit counterpart -- while adding less than 0.0003\% to GPU memory usage and incurring only a 1.7\% inference slowdown on NVIDIA RTX 4050 Mobile.
items.abstract_zh: 大型语言模型 (LLM) 的量化最近越来越受欢迎，特别是对于硬件资源有限的设备上设置。量化虽然高效，但不可避免地会降低模型质量，尤其是在 3 位和 4 位精度等激进的低位设置中。在本文中，我们提出了 DecDEC，这是一种推理方案，可以提高低位 LLM 的质量，同时保留量化的关键优势：节省 GPU 内存和减少延迟。 DecDEC 将残差矩阵（全精度权重与量化权重之间的差异）存储在 CPU 中，并动态获取一小部分权重的残差。该部分对应于显着通道，由激活异常值标记，获取的残差有助于纠正这些通道中的量化误差。通过分析输入激活，在每个解码步骤动态识别显着通道——这使得能够适应激活分布的动态特性，从而最大化误差补偿的有效性。我们通过增强最先进的量化方法来证明 DecDEC 的有效性。例如，DecDEC 将 3 位 Llama-3-8B-Instruct 模型的困惑度从 10.15 降低到 9.12，优于 3.5 位模型，同时 GPU 内存使用量增加不到 0.0003%，并且在 NVIDIA RTX 4050 Mobile 上仅导致 1.7% 的推理速度下降。

items.title: Disentangling the Dual Role of NIC Receive Rings.
Enabling Efficient GPU Communication over Multiple NICs with FuseLink.
items.abstract: Machine learning (ML) clusters stack multiple network interface cards (NICs) within each server to improve inter-server GPU communication bandwidth. However, existing systems fall short in fully utilizing NICs because of static GPU-NIC bindings. This leads to bottlenecks at hot-spot NICs when handling imbalanced communication in ML tasks. For example, large language model serving instances may have different communication demands across NICs; expert-parallel training tasks have imbalanced all-to-all traffic; and the embedding transmission volumes during recommendation model training vary across GPUs. To fully utilize all NICs, we propose FuseLink to enable efficient GPU communication over multiple NICs. FuseLink extends inter-server network by integrating high-speed intra-server connections, and leverages GPUs to efficiently relay traffic to idle NICs. We implement FuseLink and integrate it into NCCL, so that ML applications can benefit from FuseLink seamlessly without code modifications. Compared to NCCL, we demonstrate that FuseLink achieves up to 212GBps bandwidth between two inter-server GPUs and accelerates ML tasks with dynamic traffic patterns. Specifically, it reduces the latencies of first-token generation in LLM model servings by 1.04-2.73?, improves the training throughput of mixture-of-experts model by up to 1.3?, and accelerates deep learning recommendation model training by up to 1.2?.
items.abstract_zh: 机器学习 (ML) 集群在每台服务器内堆叠多个网络接口卡 (NIC)，以提高服务器间 GPU 通信带宽。然而，由于静态 GPU-NIC 绑定，现有系统无法充分利用 NIC。在处理 ML 任务中的不平衡通信时，这会导致热点 NIC 出现瓶颈。例如，大型语言模型服务实例可能具有跨 NIC 的不同通信需求；专家并行的训练任务造成了所有流量的不平衡；推荐模型训练期间的嵌入传输量因 GPU 的不同而不同。为了充分利用所有 NIC，我们建议使用 FuseLink 来实现多个 NIC 上的高效 GPU 通信。 FuseLink 通过集成高速服务器内连接来扩展服务器间网络，并利用 GPU 有效地将流量中继到空闲 NIC。我们实现了 FuseLink 并将其集成到 NCCL 中，这样 ML 应用程序就可以无缝地从 FuseLink 中受益，而无需修改代码。与 NCCL 相比，我们证明 FuseLink 在两个服务器间 GPU 之间实现高达 212GBps 的带宽，并通过动态流量模式加速 ML 任务。具体来说，它将 LLM 模型服务中第一个令牌生成的延迟减少了 1.04-2.73?，将专家混合模型的训练吞吐量提高了 1.3?，并将深度学习推荐模型训练加速了 1.2?。

items.title: Building Bridges: Safe Interactions with Foreign Languages through Omniglot.
Mako: Speculative Distributed Transactions with Geo-Replication.
XSched: Preemptive Scheduling for Diverse XPUs.
Weave: Efficient and Expressive Oblivious Analytics at Scale.
Scalio: Scaling up DPU-based JBOF Key-value Store with NVMe-oF Target Offload.
Quantum Virtual Machines.
FineMem: Breaking the Allocation Overhead vs. Memory Waste Dilemma in Fine-Grained Disaggregated Memory Management.
To PRI or Not To PRI, That's the question.
Söze: One Network Telemetry Is All You Need for Per-flow Weighted Bandwidth Allocation at Scale.
items.abstract: Weighted bandwidth allocation is a powerful abstraction that has a wide range of use cases in modern data center networks. However, realizing highly agile and precise weighted bandwidth allocation for large-scale cloud environments is fundamentally challenging. In this paper, we propose Söze, a lightweight decentralized weighted bandwidth allocation system that leverages simple network telemetry features of commodity Ethernet switches. Given the flow weights, Söze can effectively use the telemetry information to compute and enforce the weighted bandwidth allocations without per-flow, topology, or routing knowledge. We demonstrate the effectiveness of Söze through simulations and testbed experiments, improving TPC-H jobs completion time by up to $0.59\times$ and $0.79\times$ on average.
items.abstract_zh: 加权带宽分配是一种强大的抽象，在现代数据中心网络中具有广泛的用例。然而，为大规模云环境实现高度敏捷和精确的加权带宽分配具有根本性的挑战。在本文中，我们提出了 Söze，这是一种轻量级的去中心化加权带宽分配系统，它利用了商用以太网交换机的简单网络遥测功能。给定流权重，Söze 可以有效地使用遥测信息来计算和实施加权带宽分配，而无需了解每个流、拓扑或路由知识。我们通过模拟和测试台实验证明了 Söze 的有效性，将 TPC-H 作业完成时间平均提高了 0.59 美元和 0.79 美元。

items.title: ZEN: Empowering Distributed Training with Sparsity-driven Data Synchronization.
Mirage: A Multi-Level Superoptimizer for Tensor Programs.
items.abstract: We introduce Mirage, the first multi-level superoptimizer for tensor programs. A key idea in Mirage is $μ$Graphs, a uniform representation of tensor programs at the kernel, thread block, and thread levels of the GPU compute hierarchy. $μ$Graphs enable Mirage to discover novel optimizations that combine algebraic transformations, schedule transformations, and generation of new custom kernels. To navigate the large search space, Mirage introduces a pruning technique based on abstraction that significantly reduces the search space and provides a certain optimality guarantee. To ensure that the optimized $μ$Graph is equivalent to the input program, Mirage introduces a probabilistic equivalence verification procedure with strong theoretical guarantees. Our evaluation shows that Mirage outperforms existing approaches by up to 3.3$\times$ even for DNNs that are widely used and heavily optimized. Mirage is publicly available at https://github.com/mirage-project/mirage.
items.abstract_zh: 我们推出 Mirage，第一个用于张量程序的多级超级优化器。 Mirage 的一个关键思想是 $μ$Graphs，它是 GPU 计算层次结构的内核、线程块和线程级别的张量程序的统一表示。 $μ$Graphs 使 Mirage 能够发现结合代数变换、调度变换和新自定义内核生成的新颖优化。为了导航大搜索空间，Mirage 引入了基于抽象的剪枝技术，可显着减少搜索空间并提供一定的最优性保证。为了确保优化后的$μ$Graph与输入程序等价，Mirage引入了具有强大理论保证的概率等价验证程序。我们的评估表明，即使对于广泛使用和深度优化的 DNN，Mirage 的性能也比现有方法高出 3.3$\times$。 Mirage 可在 https://github.com/mirage-project/mirage 上公开获取。

items.title: OS Rendering Service Made Parallel with Out-of-Order Execution and In-Order Commit.
Decouple and Decompose: Scaling Resource Allocation with DeDe.
items.abstract: Efficient resource allocation is essential in cloud systems to facilitate resource sharing among tenants. However, the growing scale of these optimization problems have outpaced commercial solvers commonly employed in production. To accelerate resource allocation, prior approaches either customize solutions for narrow domains or impose workload-specific assumptions. In this work, we revisit real-world resource allocation problems and uncover a common underlying structure: the vast majority of these problems are inherently separable, i.e., they optimize the aggregate utility of individual resource and demand allocations, under separate constraints for each resource and each demand. Building on this observation, we develop DeDe, a scalable and theoretically rooted optimization framework for large-scale resource allocation. At the core of DeDe is a decouple-and-decompose approach: it decouples entangled resource and demand constraints and thereby decomposes the overall optimization into alternating per-resource and per-demand subproblems that can be solved efficiently and in parallel. We have implemented and released DeDe as a Python package with a familiar modeling interface. Our experiments on three representative resource allocation tasks -- cluster scheduling, traffic engineering, and load balancing -- demonstrate that DeDe delivers significant speedups while generating higher-quality allocations.
items.abstract_zh: 在云系统中，高效的资源分配对于促进租户之间的资源共享至关重要。然而，这些优化问题的规模不断扩大，已经超过了生产中常用的商业求解器。为了加速资源分配，先前的方法要么为狭窄的领域定制解决方案，要么强加特定于工作负载的假设。在这项工作中，我们重新审视现实世界的资源分配问题，并揭示了一个共同的底层结构：这些问题中的绝大多数本质上是可分离的，即它们在每种资源和每种需求的单独约束下优化单个资源和需求分配的总效用。基于这一观察，我们开发了 DeDe，一个可扩展且有理论基础的优化框架，用于大规模资源分配。 DeDe 的核心是一种解耦和分解方法：它解耦了纠缠的资源和需求约束，从而将整体优化分解为交替的每个资源和每个需求的子问题，这些子问题可以有效地并行解决。我们已经实现并发布了 DeDe 作为 Python 包，具有​​熟悉的建模界面。我们对三个代表性资源分配任务（集群调度、流量工程和负载平衡）进行的实验表明，DeDe 在生成更高质量的分配的同时提供了显着的加速。

items.title: Deterministic Client: Enforcing Determinism on Untrusted Machine Code.
KRR: Efficient and Scalable Kernel Record Replay.
Basilisk: Using Provenance Invariants to Automate Proofs of Undecidable Protocols.
BlitzScale: Fast and Live Large Model Autoscaling with O(1) Host Caching.
items.abstract: Model autoscaling is the key mechanism to achieve serverless model-as-a-service, but it faces a fundamental trade-off between scaling speed and storage/memory usage to cache parameters, and cannot meet frequent scaling requirements across multiple hosts. The key problem is that data plane performance is slow, and scaled instances remain stopped while parameters are loading. In this paper, we first show that the data plane can be made fast with no or O(1) caching by loading parameters through the compute network between GPUs because: (1) its speed is comparable to host cache and is underutilized, and (2) scaling multiple instances requires no or O(1) caching with network-optimized multicast. Second, autoscaling can be made live by breaking the scaling abstraction for inference from a coarse-grained instance-level to a fine-grained layer-level. This allows us to offload the layer computation from the overloaded serving instances to the scaled ones without waiting for the parameters to be fully loaded. Under real-world workloads, our system BLITZSCALE achieves up to 94 % lower tail latency reductions compared to state-of-the-art autoscaling system (ServerlessLLM), and it reduces the GPU time used for serving by 49 % when compared with serving systems that do not support autoscaling like DistServe and vLLM with the same service-level-agreement.
items.abstract_zh: 模型自动伸缩是实现无服务器模型即服务的关键机制，但它面临着伸缩速度和缓存参数的存储/内存使用之间的基本权衡，并且无法满足跨多个主机的频繁伸缩需求。关键问题是数据平面性能缓慢，并且在加载参数时缩放实例保持停止状态。在本文中，我们首先证明，通过通过 GPU 之间的计算网络加载参数，可以在没有缓存或 O(1) 缓存的情况下加快数据平面的速度，因为：(1) 其速度与主机缓存相当，并且未得到充分利用，(2) 扩展多个实例不需要使用网络优化的多播或 O(1) 缓存。其次，可以通过打破从粗粒度实例级别到细粒度层级别的推理的缩放抽象来实现自动缩放。这允许我们将层计算从超载的服务实例卸载到缩放的服务实例，而无需等待参数完全加载。在实际工作负载下，与最先进的自动扩展系统 (ServerlessLLM) 相比，我们的系统 BLITZSCALE 的尾部延迟降低了 94%，并且与具有相同服务级别协议的 DistServe 和 vLLM 等不支持自动扩展的服务系统相比，用于服务的 GPU 时间减少了 49%。

items.title: Extending Applications Safely and Efficiently.
NanoFlow: Towards Optimal Large Language Model Serving Throughput.
items.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have resulted in a surging demand for planet-scale serving systems, where tens of thousands of GPUs continuously serve hundreds of millions of users. Consequently, throughput has emerged as a key metric that determines serving systems' performance. Due to large model sizes and memory-intensive self-attention, LLM serving has been commonly assumed to be memory-bound. Through a detailed analysis, we show that despite having memory-intensive components, end-to-end LLM serving is compute bound for most common workloads and LLMs. Alas, most existing serving engines fall short from optimal compute utilization, because the heterogeneous operations that comprise LLM serving--compute, memory, networking--are executed sequentially within a device. We propose NanoFlow, a novel serving framework that exploits intra-device parallelism, which overlaps the usage of heterogeneous resources within a single device. NanoFlow splits inputs into smaller nano-batches and duplicates operations to operate on each portion independently, enabling overlapping. NanoFlow automatically identifies the number, size, ordering, and GPU resource allocation of nano-batches to minimize the execution time, while considering the interference of concurrent operations. We evaluate NanoFlow's end-to-end serving throughput on several popular models such as LLaMA-2-70B, Mixtral 8x7B, LLaMA-3-8B, etc. With practical workloads, NanoFlow provides 1.91x throughput boost compared to state-of-the-art serving systems achieving 50% to 72% of optimal throughput across popular models.
items.abstract_zh: 大型语言模型 (LLM) 导致了对全球服务系统的需求激增，其中数以万计的 GPU 持续为数亿用户提供服务。因此，吞吐量已成为决定服务系统性能的关键指标。由于模型尺寸较大和内存密集型自注意力机制，LLM 服务通常被认为是受内存限制的。通过详细分析，我们表明，尽管具有内存密集型组件，但端到端 LLM 服务对于大多数常见工作负载和 LLM 来说都是计算限制的。遗憾的是，大多数现有服务引擎都无法实现最佳计算利用率，因为构成 LLM 服务的异构操作（计算、内存、网络）是在设备内按顺序执行的。我们提出了 NanoFlow，一种利用设备内并行性的新颖服务框架，它与单个设备内异构资源的使用重叠。 NanoFlow 将输入分割成更小的纳米批次，并重复操作以独立地对每个部分进行操作，从而实现重叠。 NanoFlow自动识别纳米批次的数量、大小、顺序和GPU资源分配，以最大限度地减少执行时间，同时考虑并发操作的干扰。我们在 LLaMA-2-70B、Mixtral 8x7B、LLaMA-3-8B 等多种流行模型上评估了 NanoFlow 的端到端服务吞吐量。在实际工作负载下，NanoFlow 与最先进的服务系统相比，吞吐量提高了 1.91 倍，在流行模型中实现了最佳吞吐量的 50% 至 72%。

items.title: Compass: Encrypted Semantic Search with High Accuracy.
